desc: Xingping is a compact Li River town framed by classic Guangxi karst peaks, best for slow-stay scenery, dawn viewpoints, and quieter nights than Yangshuo town.
hls: Iconic Li River karst scenes around the famous 20 RMB viewpoint zone
Quieter evenings than central Yangshuo while keeping easy day-trip access
Strong sunrise/sunset photography options from nearby ridge viewpoints
Simple e-bike and boat links to river villages and countryside lanes

todo.name: Laozhai Hill sunrise climb
todo.desc: Start pre-dawn and allow time for steps that can be slick after overnight humidity.

todo.name: Li River raft segment
todo.desc: Choose licensed raft sections operating that day, as routes can shift with river level controls.

todo.name: Xingping old street wander
todo.desc: Explore preserved lanes, small docks, and courtyard guesthouses away from tour-bus peaks.

todo.name: Xianggong Mountain golden hour
todo.desc: Arrive early for tripod space and layered karst light before group tours arrive.

todo.name: Countryside e-bike loop
todo.desc: Ride river lanes to nearby hamlets and orchards, then return before dark on rural roads.

todo.name: Beer fish + rice noodle night
todo.desc: Try local beer fish styles and Guilin rice noodles in small family-run restaurants.

wifi.note: Fine in newer guesthouses, but older riverside inns can be inconsistent.

prac.fltNote: There are typically no practical UK/Ireland nonstops to Guilin region: most itineraries connect via major Chinese or regional hubs (commonly Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, Shenzhen, or Hong Kong), then continue to Xingping by road or high-speed rail plus final transfer. Guilin Liangjiang (KWL) is the closest airport, while Guangzhou (CAN) and Hong Kong (HKG) are realistic fallback entry points when fares or schedules are better. Domestic demand spikes around May Day, summer school holidays, and National Day Golden Week can materially raise final-leg prices and crowding.
prac.lang: Mandarin Chinese is the primary language; English support is limited outside larger hotels and transport nodes.
prac.tz: China Standard Time (UTC+8) year-round; no daylight saving time.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Li River water-level controls in wet months can shorten or reroute raft services on short notice; reconfirm the exact dock on the day.
Viewpoint trails (including Laozhai) are steep with uneven stone steps, and become slippery after rain—carry shoes with grip.
Golden Week and major domestic holidays create sharp crowd surges in Xingping and Xianggong; book rooms and transfer tickets early.
Many small guesthouses and transport counters prioritize app payments/Chinese-language booking flows, so prepare offline payment and translation backups.

sweet: Sep-Nov and Mar-Apr for clearer karst visibility and comfortable hiking temperatures.
